channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: My Python tests passed. Production still broke.
item.description: Deployed yesterday. Tests green. Production crashed 3 hours later. The bug? A datetime object I created in tests wasn't aware of timezones. Production API expects UTC timestamps. My test just used...

item.title: Kafka Inbox/Outbox in Go — Guaranteeing Exactly-Once Delivery in a Multi-Tenant ERP
item.description: Kafka Inbox/Outbox in Go — Guaranteeing Exactly-Once Delivery in a Multi-Tenant ERP The Problem In a multi-tenant ERP handling concurrent stock and order updates, duplicate event processing is not...

item.title: How Fintech Companies Use AI IT Ops Agents Daily
item.description: Why Fintech IT Operations Break Before They Scale I've watched three fintech companies go from 50 employees to 300 in under two years. Every single one hit the same wall: their IT operations...

item.title: How I Handle Nested Tables and Rowspans (The Hard Parts of HTML Table Parsing)
item.description: Parsing HTML tables seems straightforward until you encounter real-world data. Wikipedia tables have navigation rows. Financial sites use complex rowspans. Sports statistics sites nest headers two...

item.title: DeepSource GitHub Integration: Setup and Configuration Guide
item.description: What you will learn This guide walks through every step of integrating DeepSource with GitHub - from creating your account and installing the GitHub App to configuring analyzers, transformers, and...

item.title: A YouTuber channeled his distaste for the PS5’s design into slick console covers
item.description: The PlayStation 5's curvy design is divisive. The Verge's Andrew Webster called it a "robotic clam" in his 2020 review, which is actually kind of endearing. Personally, I kind of got over hating...

item.title: Google Zanzibar Deep Dive: Handling 2 Trillion ACLs in Under 10ms
item.description: Introduction Spend any time in the authorization space and you'll notice that SpiceDB and OpenFGA both claim to be "inspired by Google Zanzibar." I got curious and pulled up the 2019 USENIX ATC...

item.title: Why we built a programming language for AI prompts instead of another GUI
item.description: The first version of our AI product had this in the codebase: system_prompt = f""" You are a customer support agent for {company}. {premium_instructions if tier == 'premium' else...

item.title: Made a new software: BetterYoutube Desktop!
item.description: What is BetterYoutube? BetterYoutube is a multiplatform, desktop youtube client made with electron, but with adblocker, and userscript built in the app. The app supports Windows, Debian-based...

item.title: (Avengers Already Warned You) CS Isn’t Dead. You’re Just Watching It Mutate
item.description: Okay so let’s drop the fake motivation for a second. The shift is real and yeah - it’s uncomfortable (as hell). People are getting laid off. Tools are evolving faster than we can even process....

item.title: I built an open-source project OpenHarness🪼
item.description: I built OpenHarness — an open-source terminal coding agent with 17 tools and 16 slash commands. It works with Ollama (free, local), OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Deepseek, Qwen or any...

item.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
item.description: Google partnered with the Brazilian government on a satellite imagery map to help protect the country’s forests.

item.title: Nintendo is weathering the storm
item.description: Things aren't going so well in the video game industry. Consoles are getting more expensive, games are shutting down, layoffs are prevalent, the live-service space is a mess, major platforms are...

item.title: Today is the final day to save up to $150 on a PS5 before the price goes up
item.description: The cost of the PlayStation Portal and every PlayStation 5 console is going up tomorrow, April 2nd, by as much as $150 in some cases. That means time is running out if you want to save yourself...

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
item.description: March 2026 AI Recap showing new updates

item.title: Block the Prompt, Not the Work: The End of "Doctor No"
item.description: There is a character that keeps appearing in enterprise security departments, and most CISOs know exactly who that is. It doesn’t build. It doesn’t enable. Its entire function is to say "No." No...

item.title: AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
item.description: If you're tired of controlling Stream Deck devices by manually pushing buttons, then good news: Elgato will now let you delegate that task to a chatbot instead. The Stream Deck 7.4 software update...

item.title: Casbaneiro Phishing Targets Latin America and Europe Using Dynamic PDF Lures
item.description: A multi-pronged phishing campaign is targeting Spanish-speaking users in organizations across Latin America and Europe to deliver Windows banking trojans like Casbaneiro (aka Metamorfo) via...

item.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The gig workers who are training humanoid robots at home...

item.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
item.description: Microsoft is calling attention to a new campaign that has leveraged WhatsApp messages to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBS) files. The activity, beginning in late February 2026,...

item.title: New Chrome Zero-Day CVE-2026-5281 Under Active Exploitation — Patch Released
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address 21 vulnerabilities, including a zero-day flaw that it said has been exploited in the wild. The high-severity...

item.title: The gig workers who are training humanoid robots at home
item.description: When Zeus, a medical student living in a hilltop city in central Nigeria, returns to his studio apartment from a long day at the hospital, he turns on his ring light, straps his iPhone to his...

item.title: 3 Reasons Attackers Are Using Your Trusted Tools Against You (And Why You Don’t See It Coming)
item.description: For years, cybersecurity has followed a familiar model: block malware, stop the attack. Now, attackers are moving on to what’s next. Threat actors now use malware less frequently in favor of...

item.title: Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
item.description: Numerous robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant Baidu froze in a major city on Tuesday, reportedly trapping passengers inside, stranding them on highways, and causing at least one accident in...

item.title: Watching a 7.5-Hour Movie in Theaters Made Me More Hopeful About Our Collective Brain Rot
item.description: Sátántango is considered a holy rite for hardcore cinephiles. In a packed screening with no cellphones in sight, it also offered a salve for brain rot.

item.title: A Taxonomy of Cognitive Security
item.description: Last week, I listened to a fascinating talk by K. Melton on cognitive security, cognitive hacking, and reality pentesting. The slides from the talk are here, but—even better—Menton has a long...

item.title: I Asked ChatGPT What WIRED’s Reviewers Recommend. Its Answers Were All Wrong
item.description: Want to know what our reviewers have actually tested and picked as the best TVs, headphones, and laptops? Ask ChatGPT, and it'll give you the wrong answers.

item.title: Google Attributes Axios npm Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Group UNC1069
item.description: Google has formally attributed the supply chain compromise of the popular Axios npm package to a financially motivated North Korean threat activity cluster tracked as UNC1069. "We have attributed...

item.title: Falcon Perception
Claude Code Source Leaked via npm Packaging Error, Anthropic Confirms
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday confirmed that internal code for its popular artificial intelligence (AI) coding assistant, Claude Code, had been inadvertently released due to a human error. "No sensitive...
